title: Why I Changed My Mind About Hydrogen Water (with Tyler LeBaron)

chunks.title: Introduction to Hydrogen Research
chunks.summary: The podcast begins with an overview of the extensive research on molecular hydrogen, highlighting its potential benefits for various health conditions, including metabolic syndrome, Parkinson's disease, and Alzheimer's. The dialogue suggests that hydrogen is a unique molecule with the ability to neutralize harmful free radicals.
chunks.transcript: What conditions has hydrogen inhalation, molecular hydrogen through water, what conditions has this been studied for? >> There's around 3,000 publications so far, over 200 human studies. Parkinson's disease, diabetes, metabolic syndrome, stroke, cancer, obviously Alzheimer's, dementia. >> You gave hydrogen water to people for 6 months and they saw improvements in multiple markers that give us indication of metabolic syndrome. >> We found that the subjects drinking hydrogen water had improved glucose levels. In fact, there was even a loss of weight, you know, improved BMI. Hydrogen is very different because it actually does not have the physical chemical property to neutralize these other free radicals. Hydrogen can only react or only neutralize the most toxic free radicals. >> It almost feels divine and it's like a divine molecu. It's wild. >> God did make the world with a word. That word would be hydrogen. >> On this week's podcast, I talked to Dr. Tyler Learon. He's probably one of the world's leading experts on hydrogen. We go into hydrogen water, inhaled hydrogen. We go into structured water, alkaline water. Are these real? Are they a scam? We talk about how much to dose the water, the protocols. We talk about the benefits, what it's actually doing in your body. I'm not really a biohacker. I just prefer to go out in the ocean, go barefoot, and surf. But I've gotten really interested in this recently, and some of the research is pretty impressive. So, I think you guys are really going to find this one interesting. If you're skeptical of hydrogen water, I think you may be changed in your mind at the end of this podcast. So stick with this one. It's very technical, but I tried to break down the parts that are more technical during the podcast. If you have questions, put them below in the comments. My team will try and answer them or I can answer them on YouTube or wherever you're watching this podcast. We're going to try and put stuff on the screen to make the technical things more digestible. But this podcast is worth

chunks.title: Expert Interview: Dr. Tyler Learon
chunks.summary: The host introduces Dr. Tyler Learon, a leading expert on molecular hydrogen, discussing his background in physiology and its implications for health. They delve into the ways that hydrogen therapy has been studied for various diseases, emphasizing the promising results of clinical studies.

It describes a significant 24-week human trial as a key example.

They explore the chemistry of hydrogen, including its ability to act as an antioxidant while also emphasizing the importance of redox balance in cellular health.

chunks.title: Hydrogen and Exercise Performance
chunks.summary: The benefits of hydrogen in relation to exercise are touched upon, with references to studies showing enhanced recovery and performance among athletes. The discussion includes the possibility of hydrogen acting as a mild exercise mimetic, highlighting the effects on oxidative stress during physical activity.

chunks.title: Hydrogen Production and Devices

chunks.title: The Future of Hydrogen Research
chunks.summary: The podcast concludes with a look ahead at ongoing research into molecular hydrogen and potential applications in healthcare. Dr. Learon encourages listeners to stay informed about hydrogen and its benefits, and notes the need for continued clinical research.
